# Review: deepmind/460.lean ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 460

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 460`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/460`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Er77c]` with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos460 ... end Erdos460`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos460`.
- [x] `open` statements (`open Finset Filter Classical`) appear **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `erdos460Seq` has a LaTeX docstring describing the greedy coprime-complement sequence.
- [x] `erdos460Sum` has a LaTeX docstring describing the partial sum.
- [x] Both definitions are marked `noncomputable` (they use `sInf` / classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function names use `lowerCamelCase`: `erdos460Seq`, `erdos460Sum`.
- N/A: No local notation needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_460` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing the source paper `[Er77c]` and stating the problem in English.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks: "Does $\sum 1/a_i \to \infty$?" ‚ÄĒ this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`, where `P = Tendsto erdos460Sum atTop atTop`.
- [x] `P` formalizes the question as originally asked (whether the sum diverges to infinity).
- [x] Problem is unsolved, so `answer(sorry)` is correct.
- [x] No quantifiers appear as theorem parameters; the limit statement is self-contained in `P`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid (`research open`).
- [x] Open problem contains `sorry` in proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]`: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses the multi-line `by` / `sorry` form (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The file contains only the single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚ąÄ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚ÜĒ`, `‚Č§`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified in this review.

---

**Summary:** The file is fully conformant with the checklist. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, custom definitions, theorem statement, `answer()` usage, category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all pass. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1) and uses `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ Tendsto erdos460Sum atTop atTop` accordingly. Both helper definitions (`erdos460Seq`, `erdos460Sum`) are well-documented and correctly marked `noncomputable`. No issues found.

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 460
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/460](https://www.erdosproblems.com/460)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThis problem arose in work of Eggleton, ErdŇĎs, and Selfridge.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[Er77c] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on combinatorial number theory III*,
    27‚ÜíNumber Theory Day (Proc. Conf., Rockefeller Univ., New York, 1976) (1977), 43‚Äď72.
    28‚Üí-/

    32‚Üínamespace Erdos460
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- The greedy coprime-complement sequence for a given $n$.
    35‚Üí$a_0 = 0$ and $a_{k+1}$ is the least integer greater than $a_k$ such that
    36‚Üí$\gcd(n - a_{k+1}, n - a_i) = 1$ for all $i \leq k$. -/

    38‚Üí  | 0 => 0
    39‚Üí  | k + 1 => sInf {m : ‚Ąē | erdos460Seq n k < m ‚ąß
    40‚Üí      ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, i ‚Č§ k ‚Üí Nat.Coprime (n - m) (n - erdos460Seq n i)}
    41‚Üí

    43‚Üísequence. -/
    44‚Üínoncomputable def erdos460Sum (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    45‚Üí  ((Finset.range n).filter (fun k =>
    46‚Üí    0 < erdos460Seq n k ‚ąß erdos460Seq n k < n)).sum
    47‚Üí    (fun k => (1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (erdos460Seq n k : ‚ĄĚ))
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/--

    54‚Üíinteger greater than $a_k$ making $\gcd(n - a_{k+1}, n - a_i) = 1$ for all
    55‚Üí$i \leq k$?

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 460

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/460](https://www.erdosproblems.com/460)

This problem arose in work of Eggleton, ErdŇĎs, and Selfridge.

[Er77c] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on combinatorial number theory III*,
Number Theory Day (Proc. Conf., Rockefeller Univ., New York, 1976) (1977), 43‚Äď72.
-/

open Finset Filter Classical

namespace Erdos460

/-- The greedy coprime-complement sequence for a given $n$.
$a_0 = 0$ and $a_{k+1}$ is the least integer greater than $a_k$ such that
$\gcd(n - a_{k+1}, n - a_i) = 1$ for all $i \leq k$. -/
noncomputable def erdos460Seq (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
  | 0 => 0
  | k + 1 => sInf {m : ‚Ąē | erdos460Seq n k < m ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, i ‚Č§ k ‚Üí Nat.Coprime (n - m) (n - erdos460Seq n i)}

/-- The sum $\sum_{0 < a_i < n} 1/a_i$ for the greedy coprime-complement
sequence. -/
noncomputable def erdos460Sum (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
  ((Finset.range n).filter (fun k =>
    0 < erdos460Seq n k ‚ąß erdos460Seq n k < n)).sum
    (fun k => (1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (erdos460Seq n k : ‚ĄĚ))

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 460 [Er77c]:

Does $\sum_{0 < a_i < n} 1/a_i \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$, where
$(a_k)$ is the greedy sequence with $a_0 = 0$ and each $a_{k+1}$ the least
integer greater than $a_k$ making $\gcd(n - a_{k+1}, n - a_i) = 1$ for all
$i \leq k$?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_460 :
    answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ Tendsto erdos460Sum atTop atTop := by
  sorry

end Erdos460

content.text: The review passed ‚ÄĒ summary says "The file is fully conformant with the checklist. No issues found." No changes needed.
